---
title: "Resources"
---

This section highlights open research artifacts produced in Spark Lab, including datasets, scripts, reproducibility material, and example results connected to our publications.

::: {.resource-intro}
These resources are meant to support reuse, reproducibility, and follow-up research. Each entry is designed to connect an artifact to the paper, measurements, scripts, or outputs that make the work easier to understand and build on.
:::

## What You Will Find Here

::: {.resource-pillars}
::: {.resource-pillar}
### Open artifacts

Public datasets, scripts, and reproducibility material released by the lab.
:::

::: {.resource-pillar}
### Research context

Each resource is tied to a paper, project, or experimental contribution.
:::

::: {.resource-pillar}
### Example outputs

Representative figures or results that help others understand how the artifact can be used.
:::
:::

## Available Resources

::: {.resource-card}
### [LoRaWAN Measurement Campaigns in Lebanon](lorawan-measurements-lebanon.qmd)

Open LoRaWAN propagation measurements collected in indoor, suburban, urban, and rural environments in Lebanon, used to derive path-loss models and evaluate LPWAN coverage.

`LPWAN` `LoRaWAN` `Propagation` `Measurement`

[View resource](lorawan-measurements-lebanon.qmd)
:::

::: {.resource-card}
### [Wi-Fi RFFI Signal-Level Synthetic Traces](wifi-rffi-signal-level-synthetic.qmd)

Synthetic Wi-Fi signal traces generated at the bit level with hardware impairments, designed for RFFI-based impersonation detection research.

`RFFI` `Wi-Fi` `Synthetic` `Impersonation Detection`

[View resource](wifi-rffi-signal-level-synthetic.qmd)
:::

## For Lab Members

Team members can contribute new datasets, scripts, notebooks, and reproducibility
material through the GitHub repository:

[https://github.com/samerlahoud/spark-lab-site](https://github.com/samerlahoud/spark-lab-site)

Start by describing the material in a new folder under `artifacts/`. When the
material is ready for the public website, add a matching page under `resources/`
and link it from this page.

[Contribution guide](contribute.qmd)
